commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

commit.message:
fix: re-fit topology when iframe becomes visible (ResizeObserver)

When AAM loads inside a hidden iframe (display:none), the container has
zero dimensions and network.fit() has no effect. Added a ResizeObserver
that triggers redraw + fit when the container first gets real dimensions.

commit.message:
Fix onboarding modal disappearing in iframe embed

The iframe height sync (ResizeObserver + postMessage) was expanding the
iframe to match content height, which broke position:fixed on the
onboarding modal - it would slide downward out of view because the
iframe viewport grew beyond the parent page's visible area.

Fix: delay iframe height sync until onboarding is complete. The inline
script checks localStorage for onboarding status and only starts the
ResizeObserver when done. OnboardingFlow dispatches a custom event on
dismiss to trigger sync start. This keeps the iframe at its default
700px min-height during onboarding so the fixed modal stays centered
in the visible viewport.

Also reverts OnboardingFlow to useEffect pattern to avoid SSR hydration
mismatch (server renders show=false, client needs show=true for
first-time users).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Support repos without remote via git worktree mode (SER-41) (#24)

Add `workspace.repo` config option that enables worktree-based workspaces.
When set, WorkspaceManager uses `git worktree add` to create isolated
working copies from a local repo instead of plain directories, with
branches named after issue identifiers. Cleanup uses `git worktree remove`
while preserving branches as records of work.

commit.message:
[ci] Adding reproduction steps in CI if failure (#3855)

Adding reproduction steps in GitHub steps:

Works locally:
```
python3 ./build_tools/github_actions/print_test_reproduction_steps.py \
        --run-id 12345678 \
        --repository ROCm/TheRock \
        --amdgpu-family gfx942 \
        --test-script "python build_tools/github_actions/test_executable_scripts/test_rocblas.py" \
        --shard-index 1 \
        --total-shards 4 \
        --test-type full \
        --fetch-artifact-args="--tests"

     ============================================================
     TEST FAILURE - REPRODUCTION STEPS
     ============================================================

     To reproduce this test failure locally, follow these steps:

     1. Start the Docker container:
        docker run -it \
            --ipc host \
            --group-add video \
            --device /dev/kfd \
            --device /dev/dri \
            ghcr.io/rocm/no_rocm_image_ubuntu24_04:latest /bin/bash

     2. Inside the container, set up the environment:
        curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | bash && source $HOME/.local/bin/env
        git clone https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock.git && cd TheRock
        uv venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
        uv pip install -r requirements-test.txt

     3. Install artifacts from this CI run:
        GITHUB_REPOSITORY=ROCm/TheRock python build_tools/install_rocm_from_artifacts.py \
            --run-id 12345678 \
            --amdgpu-family gfx942 \
            --tests

     4. Set environment variables and run the test:
        export THEROCK_BIN_DIR=./therock-build/bin
        export OUTPUT_ARTIFACTS_DIR=./therock-build
        export SHARD_INDEX=1
        export TOTAL_SHARDS=4
        export TEST_TYPE=full
        python build_tools/github_actions/test_executable_scripts/test_rocblas.py

     For more details, see:
     https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/blob/main/docs/development/test_environment_reproduction.md
     ============================================================
```

As this is test related and workflow tests above prove it works, adding
`skip-ci` label to preserve resources

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Claude Mac app not using work profile in Cowork mode

Add _aiswitch_launchd_env() that mirrors apiswitch's env vars into
the launchd bootstrap namespace via launchctl setenv/unsetenv. Called
in _aiswitch_apply before the desktop app kill/restart, so Claude and
Codex GUI apps inherit ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN on
relaunch — enabling Cowork (and the desktop app generally) to route
through the LogiQ API endpoint instead of hardcoding api.anthropic.com.

Fix AI triage: GP for generic symptoms + preserve user location

Two fixes:

1. AI Symptom Suggestion: Updated prompt to recommend General Practice
   as primary specialty for common/generic symptoms (headaches, fatigue,
   fever, etc.) with the specialist in relatedSpecialties. Also now shows
   both the primary and first related specialty as two AI suggestions in
   the dropdown, so users can choose between GP and specialist.

2. AI NL Search Location: The AI parser was overriding the user's
   explicitly selected location (e.g., London) with a hallucinated one
   (e.g., Berlin from pain in my neck). Now the user's location
   selection always takes priority; AI location is only used when the
   user hasn't selected one.

Also cleared AI caches to ensure new prompt takes effect.

commit.message:
fix: correct zip/unzip paths for macOS universal app bundles

The -app.zip archives were created with '../ShipSafer-darwin-{arch}/'
paths causing unzip (which strips leading ../ for security) to extract
into the wrong directory. Fix by:

- cd .. before zipping so entries are 'ShipSafer-darwin-{arch}/...'
- In universal job, extract with -d "$PARENT_DIR" so files land at
  /Users/runner/work/shipsafer-ide/ShipSafer-darwin-{arch}/ShipSafer.app
  which matches what create-universal-app.ts expects from its buildDir arg

- X-Goog-FieldMask limits response to duration + staticDuration

- departureTime as RFC3339 ISO string (clamped to future as before)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

More bug hunting fixes (#82)

* Restore original parameter names in explore_dpor

Revert the max_interleavings → max_executions and
timeout_per_interleaving → timeout_per_run renames from the previous
commit, bringing explore_dpor back into naming parity with
explore_interleavings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update docs.

* Fix DPOR scheduling points for back-to-back SQL operations

Inject report_and_wait(None, thread_id) directly in _intercept_execute()
and its async counterparts after _acquire_pending_row_locks(). This forces
a DPOR scheduling point at each SQL operation without relying on sys.settrace,
which skips all code in _FRONTRUN_DIR. Without this fix, pending_io was never
flushed between back-to-back SQL calls, causing DPOR to explore only 1
interleaving. Also mark defects 1 and 3 as fixed in FRONTRUN_DEFECTS.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add regression tests for DPOR SQL scheduling points fix

Three tests in test_dpor_sql_scheduling.py:

1. test_report_and_wait_called_when_dpor_active — unit test that directly
   verifies _intercept_execute calls report_and_wait(None, thread_id) once
   per reportable SQL operation when DPOR is active.  Catches any removal of
   the scheduling-point injection.

2. test_report_and_wait_not_called_when_dpor_inactive — verifies the guard:
   when no DPOR scheduler is active, report_and_wait is never called.

3. test_orm_style_sql_more_interleavings_than_without_sql_events — integration
   test using a helper compiled with a _FRONTRUN_DIR filename (so _should_trace_file
   returns False for it, simulating Django site-packages code).  Verifies that
   detect_io=True produces more interleavings than detect_io=False, confirming
   SQL scheduling points are feeding conflicts to the Rust engine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove md file.

* Fix lint errors in test_dpor_sql_scheduling.py

Remove unused threading import and fix formatting to pass ruff checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
